metadata.name: Dyslexia Support Skill
metadata.description: Accessibility tools for reading support, text simplification, and font recommendations tailored to dyslexic users
metadata.author: Justice

skills.name: dyslexia-support
skills.description: Knowledge about dyslexia, reading challenges, phonological processing, accommodation strategies, and evidence-based interventions for text accessibility

commands.name: text-simplification
commands.description: Simplify text for readability by reducing sentence complexity, replacing difficult words, and restructuring paragraphs for dyslexic readers

reading_level.description: Target reading level (elementary, middle-school, high-school, adult)

preserve_meaning.description: Whether to strictly preserve the original meaning or allow paraphrasing

commands.name: reading-guide-generation
commands.description: Generate a structured reading guide with chunked sections, vocabulary previews, and comprehension checkpoints for a given document

document.description: The document content or path to generate a guide for

chunk_size.description: How large each reading chunk should be (paragraph, section, page)

include_vocabulary.description: Whether to include vocabulary previews for difficult words

include_checkpoints.description: Whether to add comprehension checkpoints after each chunk

commands.name: font-recommendation
commands.description: Recommend dyslexia-friendly fonts and text formatting settings based on the user's reading context and preferences

context.description: The reading context (web, print, mobile, ebook, presentation)

age_group.description: Age group of the reader (child, teen, adult)

include_formatting.description: Whether to include line spacing, margin, and color recommendations

commands.name: syllable-highlighter
commands.description: Break words into color-coded syllables to support phonological decoding, with stress markers and morpheme boundaries for multi-syllable words

text.description: The word or passage to syllabify

highlight_mode.description: How to mark syllable boundaries (color-alternating, underline, slash-separated, numbered)

show_stress.description: Whether to mark the primary stressed syllable (bold or accent mark)

show_morphemes.description: Whether to additionally mark prefix, root, and suffix boundaries

commands.name: confusion-checker
commands.description: Scan text for commonly confused letters (b/d, p/q), reversed words (was/saw), and homophones (their/there/they're) and provide visual disambiguation cues

text.description: The text to check for confusable items

check_types.description: What to check for (all, letter-reversals, word-reversals, homophones)

show_mnemonics.description: Whether to include visual memory tricks for each flagged confusion (e.g., 'bed' shape for b/d)

context_aware.description: Whether to use sentence context to suggest the correct homophone choice

commands.name: vocabulary-builder
commands.description: Create visual word maps with morpheme breakdown (prefix/root/suffix), etymology, related word families, and mnemonic images to build vocabulary through meaning rather than phonics alone

words.description: Comma-separated list of words to build vocabulary maps for

include_etymology.description: Whether to include word origin and historical meaning

include_word_family.description: Whether to show related words sharing the same root

include_mnemonic.description: Whether to generate a visual or story-based memory aid

reading_level.description: Complexity level for definitions and explanations (elementary, middle-school, high-school, adult)

commands.name: writing-scaffold
commands.description: Generate structured writing frameworks with sentence starters, transition word banks, paragraph templates, and organizational graphic organizers to support written expression

task.description: Description of the writing task (e.g., 'persuasive essay about recycling', '3-paragraph book report')

format.description: Writing format (essay, paragraph, letter, report, story, email)

include_starters.description: Whether to include sentence starter suggestions for each section

include_transitions.description: Whether to include a transition word bank organized by purpose (adding, contrasting, concluding, etc.)

include_organizer.description: Whether to include a graphic organizer (outline, mind-map, or flow chart) for planning

reading_level.description: Vocabulary level for starters and transitions (elementary, middle-school, high-school, adult)

commands.name: passage-decoder
commands.description: Chunk a reading passage into manageable segments with pre-reading vocabulary, inline decoding hints, comprehension questions per chunk, and a cumulative summary scaffold

passage.description: The passage text to decode and scaffold

chunk_strategy.description: How to split the passage (sentence, paragraph, natural-break)

pre_teach_count.description: Number of difficult words to pre-teach before each chunk (0 to skip)

include_decoding_hints.description: Whether to add inline pronunciation guides for difficult words (syllable breaks + phonetic spelling)

include_summary_scaffold.description: Whether to provide a fill-in-the-blank summary template after the full passage

commands.name: context-enrichment
commands.description: Expand terse or minimal text by adding semantically supportive words and phrases around difficult vocabulary, giving dyslexic readers more contextual clues to decode unfamiliar words — the opposite of simplification

text.description: The text to enrich with contextual support

enrichment_level.description: How much context to add (light = appositives only, moderate = appositives + semantic echoes, heavy = full contextual scaffolding)

target_words.description: Optional comma-separated list of specific words to enrich around. If omitted, automatically detects likely decoding challenges.

preserve_register.description: Whether to maintain the original text's formality level (true) or shift toward conversational language (false)

show_annotations.description: Whether to visually mark which phrases were added for context (brackets or color) so a teacher or parent can see the enrichment
